original_image_tags: The page is the reverse side of a document, showing bleed-through of typed text from the front. The bleed-through is faint but partially legible. At the top left, a list of names is visible, including: "Mr. Tolson", "Mr. Van", "Mr. deBruyn", "Mr. Gandy", "Mr. Ladd", "Mr. Michle", "Mr. Rosen", "Mr. Gatt", "Mr. E.M.", "Mr. Carver", "Mr. Robbins", "Mr. Gavino", "Mr. Graham", "Mr. Harrelson", "Mr. Huntington", "Mr. Jones", "Mr. Neaf", "Mr. Rosen", "Mr. Rice", "Mr. Tye", "Mr. Moore", "Mr. Eddy". Other scattered bleed-through text includes phrases such as "The enclosed report on a... by Colonel Robert... H. Reithelt.", "The enclosed document... attached needs...", "U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE.", and "reference - see comments... page 1 to page 3." The page has a hole punch near the top center and a torn upper right corner. There are red ink markings on the left margin.

elements.description: Three distinct blue ink stamps are visible on the page. One stamp, oriented horizontally, reads 'RECEIVED-LADD FBI' followed by 'Sep 9 9 45 AM '47'. A second stamp, oriented vertically, reads 'RECEIVED-TAMM U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE FBI' followed by 'SEP 8 8 58 AM '47'. A third stamp, oriented horizontally, reads 'RECEIVED DIRECTOR U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE FBI' followed by 'SEP 8 5 08 PM '47'. The page itself is the back of a document with some faint reversed text bleed-through.

judge.reason: The two descriptions are entirely about different visual content: A describes bleed-through text and physical damage, while B describes three blue ink stamps with specific text, dates, and times that A does not mention at all.
